content:
# Story lead - Melville night market traders
#voices-of-the-city #melville

Pitch for [[Voices of the City]] (Season 3, a Tuesday slot). The 7th Street night market traders are mostly women running food stalls after their day jobs. I want to record the rush between 18:00 and 20:00.

## Angle
- How the market kept some families afloat through load-shedding.
- Who feeds the city after dark?

## To do
- Ask [[Sipho Ndaba]] to clear a Tuesday tape day.
- Check whether [[Naledi Tau]] can rig portable mics for ambient sound.

# Story lead - Yeoville soup kitchen volunteers
#voices-of-the-city #yeoville

Lead for [[Voices of the City]]. A soup kitchen off Rockey Street has run every morning for eleven years, mostly on donations from the neighbourhood.

## Angle
- The economics of feeding 200 people a day with no funding.
- Migration and belonging in Yeoville.

## Contacts
- Coordinator introduced me through [[Palesa Mokoena]].
- Possible tie-in with [[Interview questions - Reverend Grace Moyo]].

# Story lead - Soweto taxi rank poets
#voices-of-the-city #soweto

For [[Voices of the City]]. A collective of young poets performs at the Maponya Mall taxi rank on Friday afternoons. Sharp, funny, very Jozi.

## Angle
- Oral storytelling as the original morning-drive radio.
- Could feed [[Book research - the morning drive ritual]].

## To do
- Confirm transport and a Friday tape slot with [[Sipho Ndaba]].

# Story lead - load-shedding and corner shops
#the-asha-show #load-shedding

Segment idea for [[The Asha Show]]. Spaza shops in Melville and Brixton losing stock every time the power goes. Want two or three owners on the line during the morning drive.

## Angle
- The real cost of Stage 6 for someone running a fridge on borrowed money.

## To do
- Pre-interview by phone, line up callers, brief [[Dr. Nomsa Khumalo]] on the framing.

# Story lead - Jozi water restrictions
#the-asha-show #johannesburg

Quick-turn lead for [[The Asha Show]]. Rand Water restrictions hitting Gauteng again. Want a city official plus a Melville resident who's been fixing leaks on the street herself.

## Angle
- Who actually fixes the city when the city can't?

## To do
- Chase the municipal spokesperson, hold a slot Thursday.

# Interview questions - Mandla Sithole
#the-asha-show #guest

Draft questions for [[Mandla Sithole]] (music journalist) on [[The Asha Show]].

## Opening
- You've covered SA music for two decades. What sound defines Joburg right now?

## Middle
- Amapiano went global. Did we lose anything local on the way out?
- Which artist is the press still sleeping on?

## Close
- One record every Rhythm FM listener should hear before Monday.

# Interview questions - Reverend Grace Moyo
#voices-of-the-city #guest

Draft questions for [[Reverend Grace Moyo]] (community leader) for a [[Voices of the City]] feature.

## Opening
- Your congregation sits across a dozen nationalities. How do you hold that together?

## Middle
- What does the church do that the state has stopped doing?
- Where do you see real hope in this neighbourhood?

## Close
- What would you ask the city if it were listening this morning?

# Interview questions - Rhythm FM elders
#anniversary-doc

Question bank for the [[Rhythm FM 30th Anniversary documentary]] (Nov 2026, with [[Kagiso Molefe]] and [[Sipho Ndaba]]).

## For the founding hosts
- What did Rhythm FM 94.7 sound like on day one in 1996?
- A moment you knew the station mattered to Joburg.

## For the engineers
- The studio gear story nobody else remembers.

## To do
- Cross-check names with [[Naledi Tau]].

# Book research - SA radio in the 1980s
#radio-history

Notes for the [[Book proposal on SA radio culture]] (due 2026-12-31).

## Threads
- Bush radio and pirate stations under apartheid censorship.
- How music smuggled politics past the SABC.

## Sources to chase
- Archive interviews, old programme logs.
- Possible reading tie-in: [[Reading notes - Mark Gevisser]].

# Book research - community vs commercial radio
#community-radio

Chapter notes for the [[Book proposal on SA radio culture]].

## Argument
- Community stations carry languages and stories commercial radio drops.
- Where does Rhythm FM 94.7 actually sit on that line?

## To do
- Interview a community station manager in Soweto.
- Compare with [[Book research - the morning drive ritual]].

# Book research - the morning drive ritual
#morning-drive

Notes for the [[Book proposal on SA radio culture]], drawing on hosting [[The Asha Show]].

## Idea
- The 05:00 alarm, the commute, the voice in the car. Radio as the city waking up together.
- What changes when listeners stream instead of tune in?

## To do
- Track my own week of pre-dawn prep as a diary chapter.

# Off-Mic pilot outline
#off-mic #pre-launch

Outline for the [[Off-Mic podcast]] pilot (launch 2027-03-30).

## Premise
- The conversations that happen after the mic is off - longer, unhurried, behind the scenes of radio.

## Pilot guest
- Open with [[Sipho Ndaba]] on twenty years of producing live radio.

## To do
- Book studio time with [[Naledi Tau]], lock cover art before September.

# The Asha Show - segment ideas
#the-asha-show #ideas

Rolling list of segment ideas for [[The Asha Show]] morning drive.

## Recurring
- Friday listener voice notes from across Gauteng.
- A two-minute Joburg history bite each morning.

## One-offs
- Heritage Month spotlight on home languages.
- Tie a segment to [[Story lead - Melville night market traders]].

# Reading notes - Sisonke Msimang
#nonfiction #msimang

Reading 'Always Another Country' for the [[Book proposal on SA radio culture]] and for my own voice.

## What I'm taking
- How she writes belonging without sentimentality.
- Memoir as reportage - a model for the radio book.

## Quotes to revisit
- Marked the chapters on exile and return.

Maybe pitch her to [[Dr. Nomsa Khumalo]] as a guest.

# Reading notes - Mark Gevisser
#nonfiction #gevisser

Reading Gevisser on Johannesburg and its hidden geographies.

## What I'm taking
- The city as a set of overlapping, secret maps - useful for [[Voices of the City]].
- Reportage that sits with people for years, not minutes.

## To do
- Pull two passages for [[Book research - SA radio in the 1980s]].

# Reading notes - Damon Galgut
#fiction #galgut

Re-reading 'The Promise'. Not research exactly, but it sharpens how I listen.

## What I'm taking
- The shifting narrator - whose voice gets to tell the country's story.
- Silence and what goes unsaid on air.

## Note
- Talked it over with [[Palesa Mokoena]] on the weekend.
